final_prompt.prompt:
Create an original, non-infringing museum-style Chinese infographic illustration of traditional Chinese ethnic minority costume, structured as a scholarly garment atlas plate.

Style/medium: needs a "国家博物馆展板 / 历史服饰图鉴 / 文博专题信息图" aesthetic — exact stylistic execution is yours; must feel scholarly, restrained, collectible — not poster, not fashion photo, not e-commerce, not anime
Composition: must be a full vertical infographic layout with the following fixed zones:
  Top zone: Chinese main title + subtitle + introductory caption text
  Left zone: structural exploded-view diagram with Chinese leader-line annotations on key garment components, plus close-up detail insets
  Upper-right zone: material / craft / texture swatches with Chinese descriptive labels
  Mid-right zone: pattern / color / symbolism zone — primary color palette chips, pattern samples, and cultural meaning annotations in Chinese
  Bottom zone: dressing sequence / construction flow diagram + core characteristics summary panel
  Center: full-body standing figure of a person wearing the complete costume (realistic, dignified, museum-mannequin quality)
Subject: must include a full-body realistic human figure in complete traditional Chinese ethnic minority costume as the central subject; the specific ethnic group, garment system, key components, construction details, and era style are to be determined by the model based on visual richness and structural complexity — exact ethnic selection is yours
Scene/backdrop: must be a flat paper-texture background in 米白 / 绢纸白 / 浅茶色 tones — matte, archival, no gradients, no decorative scenery
Lighting/mood: needs a flat, even, archival-document atmosphere — exact light treatment is yours; no dramatic shadows, no studio glamour
Color palette: needs a muted, historically grounded direction anchored to the chosen ethnic costume's authentic colors — exact palette and ratios are yours; background must stay in 米白 / 绢纸白 / 浅茶色 range
Materials/textures: needs authentic textile surface treatment for each material swatch — exact grain, weave, sheen, and embroidery texture rendering is yours; must feel like real fabric samples, not flat fills
Text (verbatim): must include all annotation text in 简体中文 — including: main title, subtitle, introductory caption, all leader-line component labels, material swatch labels, pattern/color/symbolism annotations, dressing sequence steps, and core characteristics summary; all text must be legible, correctly formed simplified Chinese characters — no romanization, no pinyin, no English, no garbled glyphs
Constraints: must preserve all six layout zones; all in-image text must be simplified Chinese (简体中文), clear and correctly rendered; the central figure must be realistic and full-body; material swatches must look like real textile samples; annotations must be structurally accurate and connected to the correct garment parts; the overall register must be museum / scholarly / archival throughout
Avoid: poster aesthetic, portrait-studio aesthetic, e-commerce aesthetic, anime or illustration aesthetic, cosplay aesthetic, misplaced annotations, structurally incorrect garment assembly, blurry or garbled Chinese characters, fake or flat material textures, excessive decorative ornament
